records.ety: [F. populace, fr. It. popolaccio, popolazzo, fr. popolo people, L. populus. See People.]

defs: The common people; the vulgar; the multitude, -- comprehending all persons not distinguished by rank, office, education, or profession.
quotes: To . . . calm the peers and please the populace.
They . . . call us Britain's barbarous populaces.
